title: Flaky Indulgent German Sauerkraut Pastry
description: A delicious flaky pastry filled with a rich sauerkraut and score-fat filling, topped with a halal-friendly egg wash and a delicate touch of Thai fish sauce.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, and salt. Whisk until yeast is fully dissolved.
Add cold unsalted butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work the butter into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together sour cream, egg, and apple cider vinegar. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom. Trim the edges and press the dough into the corners of the pan.
In a small bowl, mix together sauerkraut, score-fat, salt, and black pepper. Spread the mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Drizzle the Thai fish sauce over the top of the sauerkraut mixture.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the egg wash over the pastry and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pastry for 35-40 minutes, or until it is golden brown.
Let the pastry cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
